articles.title: Lock Down Your Home Wi‑Fi in 10 Minutes (Beginner Guide)
articles.summary: Step-by-step checklist to secure your home router, change default passwords, and enable strong Wi‑Fi encryption in under 10 minutes.
articles.content_html:
# Lock Down Your Home Wi‑Fi in 10 Minutes

Securing your home Wi‑Fi is one of the highest‑impact things you can do as a beginner.

## Steps

1. **Change the default admin password** on your router.
2. **Enable WPA2 or WPA3** and disable WEP.
3. **Update router firmware** to the latest version.
4. **Turn off WPS** if you don't need it.

## Next steps

If you want to go deeper, check out the follow‑up guides linked at the end of this article.

articles.title: Beginner’s Checklist for Staying Safe on Public Wi‑Fi
articles.summary: A beginner-friendly checklist to keep your accounts and data safe when using coffee shop and airport Wi‑Fi networks.
articles.content_html:
# Beginner’s Checklist for Staying Safe on Public Wi‑Fi

Public Wi‑Fi is convenient, but it comes with real risks.

## Key rules

- Avoid logging into sensitive accounts.
- Prefer **HTTPS** sites.
- Use a **VPN** if you have one.

## Quick actions

Run through this checklist before you connect to any open network.

articles.title: 5 Quick Wins to Upgrade Your Wi‑Fi Security Today
articles.summary: Five simple Wi‑Fi security tweaks any beginner can apply in under an hour, with screenshots and clear explanations.
articles.content_html:
# 5 Quick Wins to Upgrade Your Wi‑Fi Security Today

You don’t need to be a hacker to make your Wi‑Fi harder to attack.

## Quick wins

1. Rename your SSID to something non‑identifying.
2. Use a **unique, strong Wi‑Fi password**.
3. Separate guest devices onto a guest network.
4. Disable remote administration.
5. Schedule regular firmware checks.

articles.title: VPN Setup for Absolute Beginners (Desktop & Mobile)
articles.summary: A friendly VPN setup guide that walks beginners through installing and configuring a VPN on Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS.
articles.content_html:
# VPN Setup for Absolute Beginners

In this guide we’ll install a VPN client, connect to a server, and verify your traffic is actually encrypted.

## What you’ll learn

- How a VPN protects you on public Wi‑Fi.
- How to install a VPN app on desktop and mobile.
- How to confirm your IP address really changed.

## Step 1: Choose a provider

Pick any reputable VPN provider that supports your devices. The specific brand doesn’t matter for this tutorial.

articles.title: Advanced VPN Setup: Run Your VPN on the Home Router
articles.summary: How to terminate your VPN tunnel on the router so every device in your home automatically uses the VPN.
articles.content_html:
# Advanced VPN Setup on Your Home Router

Running your VPN on the router centralizes privacy for the whole house.

## Prerequisites

- A router that supports OpenVPN or WireGuard.
- Configuration files from your VPN provider.

## High-level steps

1. Backup your router config.
2. Enable the VPN client in the router UI or flash a custom firmware.
3. Import the VPN config and test DNS leaks.

articles.title: The 5 Best Password Managers for 2025 (Hands‑On Review)
articles.summary: A practical, hands‑on review of the top 5 password managers in 2025, with threat‑model‑aware recommendations.
articles.content_html:
# The 5 Best Password Managers for 2025

Password managers are still the easiest way to upgrade your account security.

## Recommended tools

We spent weeks testing popular options and narrowed them down to a shortlist.

- Bitwarden
- 1Password
- KeePassXC
- Proton Pass
- Dashlane

We’ll compare UX, security model, and export options so you can choose safely.

articles.title: 3 Password Managers I Actually Trust as a Hacker
articles.summary: Opinionated review of three password managers that hold up under a hacker’s threat model and code‑review scrutiny.
articles.content_html:
# 3 Password Managers I Actually Trust

After years of testing breach reports and poking at sync protocols, these three stood out.

## Recommended tools

- Bitwarden
- 1Password
- KeePassXC

We’ll walk through why each one earned a spot and when you might prefer one over another.

articles.title: Lightweight Password Manager Roundup for Minimalists
articles.summary: Comparing 4 lightweight password managers that don’t try to be everything apps and focus on simplicity.
articles.content_html:
# Lightweight Password Manager Roundup

If you hate bloated software, these tools keep things simple.

## Recommended tools

- KeePass
- KeePassXC
- KeeWeb
- Enpass

We’ll look at sync options, offline support, and how each handles encryption keys.

articles.title: Spot Phishing Emails in 5 Minutes: A Visual Guide
articles.summary: Short, visual guide to spotting phishing emails using real redacted examples, ideal for quick training.
articles.content_html:
# Spot Phishing Emails in 5 Minutes

This article walks through real‑world phishing emails and highlights the red flags.

## Common red flags

- Mismatched sender display name vs. address
- Urgent language combined with login links
- Attachments you didn’t ask for

Use this as a pre‑meeting warm‑up or a quick refresher.

articles.title: 11 Red Flags That Scream Phishing
articles.summary: A quick reference list of 11 phishing red flags you can teach to non‑technical friends and family.
articles.content_html:
# 11 Red Flags That Scream Phishing

Even if you never touch a packet sniffer, you can spot most phishing with simple checks.

We’ll walk through 11 red flags and show how they appear in real messages.

articles.title: CTF Write‑up: "Cookie Jar" Web Challenge (2024, Intermediate)
articles.summary: Intermediate‑level web challenge write‑up from a 2024 CTF, focusing on cookie tampering and auth bypass.
articles.content_html:
# CTF Write‑up: "Cookie Jar" (2024)

This intermediate web challenge revolves around insecure cookie handling.

## Challenge overview

You’re given a simple login form and a single `session` cookie. The goal is to escalate from a regular user to admin.

We’ll walk through enumeration, decoding, tampering, and the final payload.

articles.title: CTF Write‑up: Hunting for SSTI in a Python Blog (2024, Intermediate)
articles.summary: Intermediate 2024 CTF web write‑up centered on discovering and exploiting server‑side template injection.
articles.content_html:
# CTF Write‑up: SSTI in a Python Blog (2024)

This write‑up covers a server‑side template injection bug in a minimalist blog engine.

## Approach

We’ll start with input discovery, then move to template syntax fuzzing and finally remote code execution.

articles.title: Web App Penetration Testing, Part 1: Mindset, Scope & Prerequisites
articles.summary: Part 1 of the Web App Penetration Testing series: mindset, rules of engagement, lab setup, and prerequisites.
articles.content_html:
# Web App Penetration Testing, Part 1

Welcome to Part 1 of the Web App Penetration Testing series.

## Prerequisites

Before you start, you should:

- Be comfortable with basic HTTP requests and responses.
- Know how to run a Linux VM (or WSL) on your machine.
- Have a test target you are explicitly allowed to attack.

## Scope and rules of engagement

We’ll define scope, authorization, and what is explicitly out‑of‑bounds before touching any target.

articles.title: Web App Penetration Testing, Part 2: Recon & Mapping the Attack Surface
articles.summary: Part 2 of the Web App Penetration Testing series: recon strategies, content discovery, and mapping the app.
articles.content_html:
# Web App Penetration Testing, Part 2

In this part we’ll map the app before we touch any exploits.

We’ll cover content discovery, directory brute forcing, and how to build a living map of the target.

articles.title: Web App Penetration Testing, Part 3: Input Validation & Auth Bugs
articles.summary: Part 3 dives into testing input validation, authentication, and session handling in web apps.
articles.content_html:
# Web App Penetration Testing, Part 3

Now we start poking at how the app trusts input and identifies users.

We’ll work through common auth and session issues with practical examples.

articles.title: Web App Penetration Testing, Part 4: Exploiting Injection Flaws
articles.summary: Part 4 focuses on finding and exploiting injection vulnerabilities such as SQLi, XSS, and template injection.
articles.content_html:
# Web App Penetration Testing, Part 4

In this part we’ll focus on classic injection bugs.

We’ll walk through SQL injection, XSS, and template injection using a lab application.

article_comments.content: This was the first VPN guide that didn’t confuse me, great screenshots and pacing.
Followed this on both my laptop and phone and everything worked exactly as described.
Really appreciated the explanation of what a VPN does before diving into steps.
Can confirm the IP check step works, helped me verify my tunnel was actually active.
Nice beginner level write up, maybe add a short section about DNS leaks next time.
Got my OpenVPN client running on an old router thanks to this guide, super detailed steps.
The warning about backing up router config first probably saved me from a headache.
Flashed OpenWrt and followed your WireGuard section, whole house is now on the VPN.
Some router screenshots differ a bit from mine but the concepts translated perfectly.
Appreciate the section on testing DNS leaks and kill switches at the very end.
Had to tweak MTU settings but this article gave me enough context to troubleshoot it.
This is the clearest explanation of router based VPN I have found so far anywhere.
Please add a short section on dual WAN setups, otherwise this is an excellent guide.
Great visuals, I used this as a quick training piece for my non technical relatives.
Love how short this is while still covering the most important phishing red flags.
Helpful checklist style article, would be nice to have a printable PDF version too.
Clear walkthrough of the cookie tampering steps, especially the decoding phase.
I used this write up while reviewing my own solution and caught a missed edge case.
Exactly the kind of practical comparison I needed to choose a password manager.
The prerequisites section is gold, especially the emphasis on legal scope and rules.
